title: Implement bounded finding evaluation

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows pending

commands.name: Missing bounded evaluator
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/finding-evaluation.test.ts --reporter=dot
commands.expectedFailure: The decision-table suite failed to import the not-yet-created finding-evaluation module

commands.name: Missing bounded evaluator properties
commands.expectedFailure: The fixed-seed property suite failed to import the not-yet-created finding-evaluation module

commands.name: Shrunk whitespace counterexamples
commands.expectedFailure: Fast-check shrank invalid model narrative and missing-coverage text to a single whitespace character; both cases were preserved as permanent rejection examples

commands.name: Decision table and four bounded properties
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/finding-evaluation.test.ts --project property tests/property/finding-evaluation.property.test.ts --reporter=dot

commands.name: Missing immutable finding repositories
commands.expectedFailure: The in-memory repository did not exist and the first PostgreSQL fixture attempted to bypass the run state machine before persistence was implemented

commands.name: In-memory and PostgreSQL append-only finding persistence
Missing authenticated findings API
commands.expectedFailure: The focused HTTP suite failed to import the not-yet-created workspace findings route

commands.name: Undefined finding-state browser stories
commands.expectedFailure: Five scenarios exposed twenty-six undefined finding-matrix steps before the API, UI, and browser bindings existed; the stale core build also made the first fixture reset fail until the package was rebuilt

commands.name: All bounded outcomes through the real browser and API
Focused evaluator, repository, route, and run-fixture matrix
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/finding-evaluation.test.ts --project property tests/property/finding-evaluation.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/finding-evaluation.test.ts apps/web/tests/integration/finding-evaluation-routes.test.ts tests/integration/run-orchestration.test.ts --reporter=dot

commands.name: Repository lint, type, optimized build, and production dependency audit
commands.command: pnpm lint; pnpm typecheck; pnpm build; pnpm audit --prod

commands.name: Source-bound optimized-production finding matrix capture
commands.command: PACTWIRE_CAPTURE_CURATED_EVIDENCE=1 PACTWIRE_EVIDENCE_TASK=DET-03 PACTWIRE_BDD_TAGS='@DET-03 and @FR-046' pnpm test:bdd -- --production

commands.name: Evidence inventory recognized DET-03 proof
commands.command: pnpm evidence:check; pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts --reporter=dot
commands.expectedFailure: Traceability accepted the new manifest at twenty-one manifests and 206 proof files while the explicit inventory regression still expected the previous twenty manifests and 196 proof files

commands.name: Completed DET-03 evidence traceability and inventory
commands.command: pnpm evidence:check; pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts --reporter=dot

scenarios.scenario: A complete named run reports only what its deterministic evidence supports
A confirmed prohibited flow is shown as a bounded recorded conflict
A repaired rerun stays linked to its prior conflict without claiming approval
Missing visibility remains distinct from a clean named run
Unsupported traffic to an unknown destination requires human review

assets.altText: Desktop Pactwire finding-state matrix showing all six bounded results beside a selected recorded-conflict detail with named scope, path coverage, deterministic evidence, separated model explanation, and limitations.
assets.caption: Direct-language state cards lead with meaning rather than internal tokens; the selected detail identifies exactly what controlled the result and what the result does not establish.

assets.altText: Narrow Pactwire finding-state matrix stacking all six bounded results and the same selected recorded-conflict evidence without horizontal overflow or hidden scope.
assets.caption: The narrow layout preserves every state, scope boundary, deterministic evidence reference, model disclaimer, and limitation in reading order.

proofs.description: Strict input and output schemas, ordered deterministic evaluator, six bounded states, direct copy, immutable repositories, and exact run-manifest binding
Decision-table examples for every state and uncertainty class, bounded positive scope, prohibited-copy guard, and preserved shrunk regressions
Fixed-seed PROP-04, PROP-05, PROP-07, and PROP-21 invariants with 500 runs each
In-memory and PostgreSQL exact-payload persistence, finalized-manifest lineage, duplicate rejection, and database mutation guards
Authenticated all-state API, machine-readable decision table, direct-copy boundary, unsigned denial, and cross-workspace concealment
No-store workspace-authorized endpoint returning only stored evaluations and the deterministic decision table
Selectable six-state matrix with named scope, coverage, deterministic basis, model separation, prior finding, reason codes, and limitations
Complete named-scope and all-state interpretation story tagged DET-03, FR-040, and FR-046
Confirmed prohibited-flow and deterministic-basis browser story tagged DET-03
Prior-conflict linkage and forbidden approval-copy browser story tagged DET-03
Distinct NOT_VISIBLE and NOT_TESTED paths through the browser tagged DET-03 and PROP-05
Unsupported transform and unknown destination remain NEEDS_REVIEW through the browser tagged DET-03, FR-041, and PROP-04
Real browser and authenticated API assertions plus source-bound desktop, narrow, and machine-table capture
Explicit repository inventory regression for twenty-one manifests and 206 unique proof files
Source-bound ordered evaluator conditions and controlled example output for every finding state
DET-03 evidence description, provenance, responsibility boundary, and non-claim

knownLimitations: The committed matrix uses controlled fictional identities, reserved domains, and synthetic observations; it does not establish effectiveness or adoption on real school software.
Purpose meaning, legal interpretation, unknown ownership, unsupported transforms, collisions, and incomplete evidence lineage remain NEEDS_REVIEW rather than being inferred by a model.
A positive conflict is limited to its deterministically matched observation and confirmed prohibited destination; unrelated partial coverage remains visible in the named scope.
The model-explanation boundary is exercised with controlled fixture prose and makes no claim about model explanation quality.
GitHub Actions clean-checkout verification is pending because the current account billing rejection prevents jobs from starting before checkout.
